post.description: Mint a one-time hosted URL the end user opens to authorize a LinkedIn connection. Credentials land server-side and never transit the API or LLM context. `purpose` defaults to `create`. Required fields are conditional on `purpose`: for `purpose=create`, `seat_id` is required (the empty seat the new account fills); for `purpose=reconnect`, `account_id` is required (the existing account to re-auth). `expires_in_seconds` (60–3600, default 900) and `redirect_url` are optional. See the create and reconnect examples below — each is a complete, valid body for its purpose.

seat_id.description: Target seat for 'create' purpose (required when purpose=create)

account_id.description: Account to reconnect for 'reconnect' purpose (required when purpose=reconnect)

purpose.description: 'create' = new account onboarding; 'reconnect' = re-auth of existing account

expires_in_seconds.description: Seconds until the link expires (60–3600, default 900)

redirect_url.description: Optional browser return URL after the hosted flow. No credential material.

create.summary: Mint a hosted link for a new connection

reconnect.summary: Mint a hosted link to re-authorize an existing account

post.description: Re-push / re-issue the pending verification challenge notification for an account. Addressed by account_id in the body (the account_id returned with the 202 checkpoint_required response). Meaningful for otp / two_factor_sms / mobile_app_approval challenges; a two_factor_app challenge has nothing to resend (the code is generated on the device) and returns resent:false rather than an error — the response never claims a resend that did not happen. Does not reset the checkpoint's expiry.

account_id.description: The account whose pending checkpoint notification should be re-sent (returned with the 202 checkpoint_required response, or from link/poll).

200.description: The current state of the mobile-app-approval challenge. object:"account" + status:"active" once approved (the account is now connected); object:"checkpoint" + status:"pending" while still waiting; status:"expired"/"failed" on timeout/failure.

post.description: Poll the pending mobile-app-approval challenge for an account to check whether the user has approved the sign-in on their LinkedIn mobile app. Addressed by account_id in the body (the account_id returned with the 202 checkpoint_required response). There is no code to submit — approve on the phone, then poll. Returns status:"pending" while waiting, status:"active" once approved, or status:"expired"/"failed" on timeout/failure. Only valid for mobile_app_approval checkpoints — use submit for code-based challenges.

post.description: Search LinkedIn members using Recruiter filters. Supply structured filters in the request body, or pass a pasted Recruiter search URL as url (mutually exclusive with all other filters). Pagination is driven by limit and cursor query params. Requires a Recruiter seat.

url.description: Paste a LinkedIn Recruiter search URL to run it directly. Mutually exclusive with all structured filters.

keywords.description: Free-text keyword filter. Boolean modifiers supported: 'developers AND product owners NOT managers'.

locale.description: LinkedIn UI language locale (e.g. 'english'). Set to match the LinkedIn interface language when results look off.

saved_filter.description: A SAVED_FILTERS parameter ID. Resolve via search/parameters?type=SAVED_FILTERS.

location_within_area.description: Search radius in miles around the location IDs supplied in the location array.

id.description: Saved search ID. Resolve via search/parameters?type=SAVED_SEARCHES.

project_id.description: Project ID the saved search belongs to. Resolve via search/parameters?type=SAVED_SEARCHES.

newest_results_only.description: When true, returns only results new since the search was last run.

saved_search.description: Run a saved Recruiter search. Overrides all other structured filters when present.

industry.description: INDUSTRY IDs to include or exclude. Resolve via search/parameters?type=INDUSTRY.

degree.description: DEGREE IDs to include or exclude. Resolve via search/parameters?type=DEGREE.

hiring_projects.description: HIRING_PROJECTS IDs to include or exclude. Resolve via search/parameters?type=HIRING_PROJECTS.

id.description: LOCATION parameter ID. Resolve via search/parameters?type=LOCATION.

priority.description: How strictly to apply this location filter.

location.description: LOCATION IDs to filter by. Each item specifies a location ID with optional priority and relocation scope.

is_selection.description: Whether this is an exact title selection.

role.description: Job title filters. Each item is either a JOB_TITLE parameter ID or free-text keywords.

skills.description: Skill filters. Each item is either a SKILL parameter ID or free-text keywords.

company.description: Company filters. Each item is either a COMPANY parameter ID or free-text keywords.

current_company.description: Filter by current company. Each item is a numeric COMPANY parameter ID with optional priority.

past_company.description: Filter by past company. Each item is a numeric COMPANY parameter ID with optional priority.

school.description: Filter by school. Each item is a numeric parameter ID with optional priority.

company_headcount.description: Company headcount ranges. Each bucket specifies a {min, max} from the allowed enumerated values.

tenure.description: Filter by total years of experience.

tenure_in_company.description: Filter by years at current company.

tenure_in_position.description: Filter by years in current role.

recently_joined.description: Filter by how recently a member joined their company. Each bucket specifies {min, max} days.

seniority.description: Seniority level filter. Values: owner, partner, cxo, vp, director, manager, senior, entry, training, unpaid.

employment_type.description: Filter by employment type. Requires Recruiter PRO.

groups.description: LinkedIn group IDs. Resolve via search/parameters?type=GROUPS.

profile_language.description: ISO 639-1 two-character language codes for the profile interface language.

spoken_languages.description: Filter by spoken languages and proficiency. Requires Recruiter PRO.

spotlights.description: Advanced Recruiter spotlight filters (e.g. open-to-work, active talent).

first_name.description: Filter by first name. Boolean modifiers supported.

last_name.description: Filter by last name. Boolean modifiers supported.

notes.description: Filter by saved note content (min length 1).

recruiting_activity.description: Filter by recruiting activity type (messages, tags, notes, projects, resumes, reviews).

timespan.description: Number of days to suppress previously viewed profiles.

hide_previously_viewed.description: Suppress profiles you have already viewed within the given timespan.

has_military_background.description: Filter to members with a military background.

past_applicants.description: Filter to members who have previously applied to your jobs.

events.description: Canonical event names this webhook subscribes to.

data.description: Field-remapping keys for the delivery payload (messaging and user sources only).

secret_prefix.description: First 8 characters of the signing secret. The full secret is never returned on edit — it is preserved unchanged.

400.description: Invalid request — malformed URL, empty events array, event-source mismatch, a change to the immutable source, or a malformed id.

value.message: A parameter failed validation.
Missing or invalid API key.

402.description: Payment required — an active subscription is needed.

value.message: An active subscription or seat is required.

404.description: Webhook not found, or one or more account_ids not found for this tenant.

value.message: The referenced resource does not exist for this tenant.

429.description: Rate limited — slow down and retry after the hinted delay.

value.message: Too many requests; retry after the hinted delay.

patch.description: Update a webhook in place. Every field is optional — a provided field replaces its value, an omitted field is left unchanged. The source is immutable and the signing secret is preserved (never re-issued).

events.description: Replace the subscribed events (whole-array). Every event must be a canonical event whose source matches the webhook's source.

request_url.description: Replace the delivery URL. Must be an HTTPS URL.

name.description: Replace the human-readable name (1–100 chars), or null to clear it.

enabled.description: Enable or disable the webhook. A disabled webhook delivers nothing.

headers.description: Replace the custom headers added to each delivery POST (whole-array).

data.description: Replace the field-remapping keys for the delivery payload (whole-array).

account_ids.description: Replace the targeted accounts (whole-array). Must be non-empty — each id must be an acc_-prefixed id owned by the tenant.

200.description: Status changes since the given cursor, oldest-first, plus the next cursor.

404.description: No such account for this tenant.

value.message: The referenced resource does not exist for this tenant.

429.description: Rate limited — slow down and retry after the hinted delay.

value.message: Too many requests; retry after the hinted delay.
An unexpected internal error occurred.

502.description: A temporary error occurred. Please try again.

value.message: A temporary error occurred. Please try again.
The service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again.
The request timed out. Please try again.

get.description: Returns the set of changes since the last known version for a connected account, enabling event-driven state sync.
